behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is primarily a handoff/orchestration behavior across ingestion/parsing/correlation modules, not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP are not required by the behavior text. Rejection gate: I reject System because the behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess or external round-trip side effects; it only requires correct in-process grouping across service-originated records.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real parser + correlator pipeline in-process on mixed-service JSON logs and assert one combined group per trace ID across services.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: correctness depends on interaction between correlation, skew-adjustment, and timeline rendering modules. REAL-INFRA is not required by the text; skew handling can be validated with controlled timestamps in-process. No LLM or agent pipeline dependency. Rejection gate: I reject System because no real HTTP/subprocess/OS primitive or external boundary readback is required by this behavior statement.
behaviors.test_description: Feed multi-service log sets with known timestamp offsets (<=500ms) through the real in-process timeline pipeline and assert final displayed order is correct.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: Unit tests likely cover percentile math; additional risk is module wiring from aggregation -> threshold logic -> anomaly report flagging. No requirement for real external infra, and no AI/ML output dependency. Rejection gate: I reject System because behavior text does not require validating external API/report contract via real boundary; internal report object/output generation can be verified in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real stats and anomaly-report modules together on synthetic latency distributions where p99 crosses and does not cross 3x baseline, asserting correct flag presence.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: This failure mode is often in module interaction: parser error handling, pipeline continuation, and metrics/reporting counter propagation. Real OS/network/subprocess is not explicitly needed; can be exercised in-process with malformed records. No AI/ML or multi-agent chain. Rejection gate: I reject System because the behavior text does not require real filesystem/network ingestion or external round-trip verification; it focuses on pipeline resilience and reported count.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full in-process ingest pipeline with mixed valid/invalid JSON entries and assert processing continues, valid entries are retained, and skipped-count matches malformed input count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: burst throughput and backpressure behavior depend on runtime scheduling, queues, I/O, and timing under real process conditions; in-process mocks can hide drops/lag. ROUND-TRIP side effect is externally visible throughput/lag under load. No LLM dependency. Rejection gate: I reject Integration because the behavior explicitly requires rate/lag guarantees (10,000/sec, no falling behind), which need real process-level observation rather than only module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run the tool as a real process with a real high-rate log source at 10,000 entries/sec, then verify end-to-end processed count equals sent count and backlog/latency stays within pass criteria over the burst window.
